_meta.note: Market-access and payer intelligence. The CMS coverage framework is grounded in CMS National Coverage Determination 200.3 (Monoclonal Antibodies Directed Against Amyloid for the Treatment of Alzheimer's Disease). Access archetypes and quantitative access assumptions are transparent modelling inputs, editable in the app. Internal decision-support only; not legal, reimbursement, or medical advice.

core_requirements: Diagnosis of MCI due to AD or mild AD dementia, both with confirmed amyloid-beta pathology
Enrolment in a CMS-approved registry collecting real-world evidence
Care delivered by clinicians able to assess, diagnose, and manage the patient, with access to ARIA-appropriate MRI monitoring
Traditional (not accelerated) FDA approval broadens coverage relative to accelerated-approval agents
cms_coverage_framework.strategic_implication: CED is the single largest gate on US revenue realisation. Every eligible patient must pass through an amyloid-confirmed diagnosis, a registry-participating and MRI-capable site, and a payer pathway. Launch velocity is a function of site enablement and registry friction far more than of demand generation.

payer_archetypes.access_posture: Covered under NCD 200.3 CED with registry participation
payer_archetypes.friction: Registry data entry burden; site MRI and specialist capacity

payer_archetypes.note: Subset of the Medicare population above; plans follow NCD but may add utilization management
payer_archetypes.access_posture: Follows NCD; variable prior-authorization and site steering
Variable; medical-benefit coverage with prior authorization and medical-necessity criteria
State-variable; often restrictive with strict criteria

value_based_contracting_options: Outcomes-based agreement tied to functional decline (CDR-SB) milestones
Finite-duration payment model leveraging Kisunla's stop-after-clearance dosing to cap payer exposure
Diagnostic-linked contracting (coverage conditioned on confirmed amyloid + registry entry)
access_strategy_pillars: Site enablement: accelerate registry onboarding, MRI scheduling, and infusion capacity at target centres
Diagnostic ecosystem: expand amyloid PET and plasma p-tau217 access to widen the top of the funnel
Total-cost-of-therapy narrative: convert limited-duration dosing into a payer-facing budget-predictability story
Real-world evidence: use CED registry data as an evidence asset for future coverage expansion and contracting
ex_us_note: Ex-US access (EMA/EU, Japan, UK NICE) follows separate HTA and reimbursement logic and is out of scope for this US-anchored model; flagged as a Phase-2/3-year roadmap workstream.
